title: veronica leiocarpa var. leiocarpa
intro: Veronica leiocarpa var. leiocarpa, also known as the hairy speedwell, is a plant species within the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) native to Australia and New Guinea. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial activities. Consuming the plant has been associated with various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, coughs, and respiratory issues. Traditionally, the hairy speedwell can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties and is often used in herbal medicine.
